codeReview.summary: The fix cleanly isolates the version cleanup to the three release-metadata files, but it does not fully restore the pre-refactor state. The worker correctly identified 0.4.60 as the baseline, then committed a diff that leaves all three files at 0.4.61 after the pre-commit hook re-bumped them, so the original blocking version-metadata issue is only partially resolved across all five affected foundation features.

issues.description: The feature requires restoring `package.json` to the pre-refactor value 0.4.60, but commit `768f735` leaves the file at 0.4.61. The transcript and handoff both acknowledge the hook re-bumped the version, so the required exact revert was not actually completed.
The Chrome manifest also remains at 0.4.61 instead of the documented pre-refactor value 0.4.60, so this file is not fully restored and the prior release-metadata failure is only partially fixed.

The Firefox manifest ends at 0.4.61 rather than 0.4.60 for the same reason. Because the fix stops one increment short of the known baseline, it does not fully satisfy the feature's restore-to-baseline requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guardrail for version-bump cleanup is not precise enough about pre-commit auto-bumps. It says workers can use `--no-verify` or merely stage relevant files, but this fix staged only the three version files and still ended with an unintended 0.4.61 auto-bump. The guidance should explicitly say to bypass hooks when an exact historical version must be preserved.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md:217-219 says version bumps are out of scope and advises `--no-verify` or staging only relevant files; the fix transcript and handoff show the worker staged only the version files, then the commit hook auto-bumped them from 0.4.60 to 0.4.61, leaving `package.json:3`, `packages/extension/manifest.json:4`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json:15` at 0.4.61.

summary: Review failed. Commit 768f735 isolates the cleanup to the three version files, but it does not fully resolve the prior scrutiny failure because the committed state is 0.4.61, not the documented pre-refactor 0.4.60. The version-bump issue therefore remains unresolved for shared-consolidate-runtime-messages, shared-consolidate-orchestrator-types, shared-consolidate-profile-types, shared-consolidate-json-rpc, and shared-refactor-orchestrator.
